rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Application Bypass with DllRegisterServer Function

rules.rule_text:
title: Application Bypass with DllRegisterServer Function
id: 06c7a5b8-24dc-482c-8203-c674a7e05c56
status: experimental
description: "DLLs that are designed to be loaded by Regsvr32 are expected to have\
  \ a \nDllRegisterServer export function implemented. Adversaries will often \nsupply\
  \ the same DLL to rundll32.exe as well. Executing the DllRegisterServer \nexport\
  \ function with rundll32.exe is tradecraft that\u2019s unique to adversary \nbehavior\
  \ and is rarely seen in legitimate scenarios. We\u2019ve observed this \nbehavior\
  \ in threats including Qbot, Ursnif, and Zloader, to name a few examples. \nPart\
  \ of the RedCanary 2024 Threat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/rundll32/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.defense_evasion
- attack.t1218
- attack.t1218.011
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \rundll32.exe
    CommandLine|contains: DllRegisterServer
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
